amber.description: Catalysts, regulatory proteins, molecular machines, receptors

black.description: Terminal products, cellular outcomes, equilibrium states, system endpoints

darkSkyBlue.description: Biochemical reactions, transport, modifications, transcription, translation

green.description: External signals, nutrient availability, environmental conditions, initial states

lightCyan.description: Metabolites, molecular complexes, cellular states, compounds

purple.description: Multi-component requirements, signal integration, convergence points

yellow.description: Decision points with alternative pathways (yes/no branching)

description: Outer membrane protein (OMP) assembly in E. coli requires the BAM (β-barrel assembly machinery) complex to insert β-barrel proteins into the outer membrane. OMPs are synthesized in cytoplasm, translocated through SecYEG to periplasm as unfolded polypeptides bound by chaperones (SurA, Skp, DegP), delivered to BAM complex (BamA + BamBCDE), and inserted into outer membrane. BamA is the central insertase, a β-barrel itself. The process requires precise recognition of β-barrel signature motifs.

AND ATP?} H -->|No| I[/Translocation Blocked/] H -->|Yes| J[SecYEG Translocon] J --> K[Polypeptide Threading] K --> L[Signal Peptidase Cleavage] L --> M[OMP in Periplasm] %% Periplasmic Chaperone Handoff M --> N{OR: Which
Chaperone?} N -->|Major - SurA| O[SurA Binding] N -->|Alternative - Skp| P[Skp Binding] N -->|Quality Control - DegP| Q[DegP Protease] %% SurA Pathway O --> R[SurA PPIase Activity] R --> S[Proline Isomerization] S --> T[OMP Partially Folded] T --> U[SurA-OMP Complex] %% Skp Pathway P --> V[Skp Trimer] V --> W[Hydrophobic Cavity] W --> X[OMP Protected from Aggregation] X --> Y[Skp-OMP Complex] %% DegP Quality Control Q --> Z{AND: OMP Misfolded
AND High Temp?} Z -->|No| AA[Chaperone Activity] Z -->|Yes| AB[Protease Activity] AB --> AC[/Degradation/] AC --> AD[/(Failed OMPs Removed)/] %% BAM Complex Recognition U --> AE[Delivery to BAM] Y --> AE AA --> AE AE --> AF[BAM Complex at OM] %% BAM Complex Assembly AF --> AG{AND: BamA + BamB
+ BamCDE?} AG -->|No - Missing Subunits| AH[Assembly Fails] AG -->|Yes - Complete| AI[/Functional BAM Complex/] AI --> AJ[BamA β-Barrel Core] AJ --> AK[BamB Substrate Recognition] AK --> AL[BamC/D/E Lipoproteins] %% β-Barrel Recognition AL --> AM{AND: β-Barrel Signature
Motif Present?} AM -->|No - Not OMP| AN(\1) AM -->|Yes - OMP Motif| AO[Substrate Captured] AO --> AP[C-terminal β-Signal] AP --> AQ[Aromatic Residues] AQ --> AR[BamA Recognition] %% Insertion Mechanism AR --> AS{AND: BamA Lateral
Gate Open?} AS -->|No| AT[Gate Closed] AS -->|Yes| AU[Lateral Gate Opening] AU --> AV[OMP Enters BamA Barrel] AV --> AW[β-Strand Insertion] AW --> AX[Strand-by-Strand Threading] AX --> AY[β-Barrel Nucleation] %% Lipid Interaction AY --> AZ{AND: Lipid Bilayer
Access?} AZ -->|No| BA[Insertion Stalled] AZ -->|Yes| BB[β-Barrel Interacts with Lipids] BB --> BC[Amphipathic β-Strands] BC --> BD[Hydrophobic Face to Lipids] BD --> BE[Hydrophilic Face to Interior] %% β-Barrel Closure BE --> BF{AND: First + Last
Strands Meet?} BF -->|No| BG[Partial Barrel] BF -->|Yes| BH[β-Barrel Closes] BH --> BI[Hydrogen Bonding] BI --> BJ[Stable β-Barrel] BJ --> BK[Release from BamA] %% Final OMP Structure BK --> BL[OMP in Outer Membrane] BL --> BM{OR: OMP
Function?} BM -->|Porin| BN[Small Molecule Transport] BM -->|Adhesin| BO[Surface Attachment] BM -->|Enzyme| BP[Catalytic Activity] BM -->|Receptor| BQ[Signal Transduction] BN --> BR([Functional OMP]) BO --> BR BP --> BR BQ --> BR %% Failed Assembly I --> BS[/(Cytoplasmic Degradation)/] AH --> BS AN --> BT[/Periplasmic Degradation/] BA --> BT BG --> BT BT --> BU([Quality Control]) %% Styling %% name: Outer Membrane Protein Assembly via BAM Complex
notes: OMP assembly demonstrates high AND gates (9) for multi-component BAM complex assembly.
scientificAccuracy: Based on structural and biochemical studies of BAM complex. All components validated.

sources.title: A novel, essential, and conserved beta-barrel protein assembly pathway in bacteria.
sources.authors: Voulhoux R, Bos MP, Geurtsen J, Manganelli R, Rem PC, Luirink J.

sources.title: Structure of BamA, a key component of the outer membrane protein assembly complex.
sources.authors: Knowles TJ, Smith CJ, Latif MA, Pallen MJ, Radford SE, Bamford M, Sandkvist M, Stathopoulos C.

sources.title: Biogenesis and Insertion of β-Barrel Outer Membrane Proteins.
